Looking at the code, the issue is on line 70. The condition `if len(recent) > limit` should be `if len(recent) >= limit`. 

When the number of recent events equals the limit, the function returns `(True, 0)` instead of `(False, 0)`. The rate limit should prevent new requests when the count has reached (not just exceeded) the limit, since `remaining` would be 0 in that case and no more requests should be allowed.
